vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves editing /etc/systemd/logind.conf to set StopIdleSessionSec and reloading systemd. This is a straightforward configuration change that does not affect authentication, networking, or filesystem structures and poses no risk of locking out users or breaking critical services.

# should be created by either modifying this file, or by creating "drop-ins" in
# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleRebootKey=reboot
#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

#  This file is part of systemd.
#
#  systemd is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the
#  terms of the GNU Lesser General Public License as published by the Free
#  Software Foundation; either version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option)
#  any later version.
#
# Entries in this file show the compile time defaults. Local configuration
# should be created by either modifying this file, or by creating "drop-ins" in
# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
StopIdleSessionSec=10min
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleRebootKey=reboot
#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192

llm_verdict.message:
Glad to hear the remediation was successful!

If you have any other OpenSCAP findings, configuration items, or security hardening tasks you’d like to address—whether they involve systemd, PAM, SELinux, firewall rules, package updates, or anything else—just let me know. I’m ready to help you inspect the relevant files, apply the appropriate fixes, and verify compliance.

llm_verdict.message:
Glad to hear the remediation was successful! The `StopIdleSessionSec=10min` setting is now in place via a drop‑in, and the systemd daemon has been reloaded, so the **logind_session_timeout** rule should now be satisfied.

If you’d like me to verify any other settings, run additional checks, or address another finding, just let me know. I'm ready to help with any further remediation tasks.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed steps will correctly configure StopIdleSessionSec and reload systemd‑logind, satisfying the OpenSCAP rule. However, the remediation is presented as manual instructions without a concrete timeout value, lacks idempotency, and does not use systemd drop‑in files or backup the original configuration, which are preferred best‑practice approaches.
concerns: No specific timeout value is defined; the operator must choose and insert it.
The instructions are manual and not scripted, increasing risk of human error.
Editing the main logind.conf directly can overwrite other custom settings.
Potential for mis‑typed units that would cause systemd‑logind to reject the entry.
suggested_improvements: Specify the required timeout value (e.g., StopIdleSessionSec=10min) in the remediation.
Implement the change as an idempotent script or using a systemd drop‑in: `systemctl edit systemd-logind` and add a `[Login]` section with the setting.
Create a backup of /etc/systemd/logind.conf before modifying it.
Add a check that the configuration parses correctly after `systemctl daemon-reload` (e.g., by inspecting the exit status).
Wrap the commands in a single, repeatable script that can be audited and rerun safely.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation correctly implements StopIdleSessionSec via a systemd drop‑in, backs up the original config, reloads the daemon and restarts logind, thereby fixing the vulnerability. It is safe and functional, though it could be packaged as an idempotent script with error handling for greater robustness.
concerns: The remediation is presented as manual steps rather than an automated, repeatable script.
Restarting systemd-logind will briefly interrupt user sessions.
No explicit error handling for daemon-reload or restart failures.
Backup only of the main file; the new drop‑in file is not backed up.
suggested_improvements: Provide a single script that performs all steps with idempotent checks.
Add verification of command exit codes and abort on failure.
Consider using `systemctl edit systemd-logind` to create the drop‑in in a more standard way.
Backup the drop‑in file before modification or include rollback instructions.
